title: Pointer-object const parameters received overlapping transformations
summary: T* const and const T* const parameters could display duplicated or misplaced const because raw-line reordering and parameter flipping both owned the same bytes.
details: The browser semantic matrix initially rendered const fixed_ptr: T^ const and even a duplicated constconst form.
aliases.const_pointer_reorder handled raw declarations while aliases.flip_param independently concealed and replaced an entire function parameter.
The fix makes raw const-pointer reordering defer inside parameter_declaration nodes and gives flip_param sole ownership there.
The resulting complete forms are const fixed: T^ and const fixed_ro: const T^.

provenance.note: The actual embedded UI exposed composition that isolated helper output did not.

evidence.claim: Both pointer const axes are rendered once and source bytes remain unchanged.

questions: Why did const T* const duplicate const in the style lab?
Which renderer owns pointer-object const inside a parameter?

last_verified.method: Failing real-grid output corrected and pinned by integrated parameter assertions.
